text: This is the VOA Special English Health Report.
zh: [0] 这里是美国之音特别英语健康报道。

text: We recently told you about a study which found that more than 10 %
zh: [1] 我们最近告诉过大家一项研究,该研究发现全世界超过10%

text: of all babies worldwide are born too early.
zh: [2] 的新生儿出生过早。

text: A common problem in preterm babies is respiratory disease.
zh: [3] 早产儿常见的问题是呼吸系统疾病。

text: The lungs are the last organs to develop.
zh: [4] 肺是最后发育成熟的器官。

text: But a medicine called surfactant can save babies struggling to breathe.
zh: [5] 但一种叫做表面活性物质的药物可以挽救呼吸困难的婴儿。

text: The story of this life -saving medicine begins with with a discovery in 1959
zh: [6] 这种救命药物的故事始于1959年的一项发现,

text: by a researcher named Mary Ellen Avery.
zh: [7] 发现者是研究员玛丽·艾伦·艾弗里。

text: She told this story in 2005 to Children's News
zh: [8] 2005年,她向《儿童新闻》讲述了这个故事,

text: at Children's Hospital Boston, where she was the first woman to serve as physician -in -chief.
zh: [9] 地点是波士顿儿童医院,她是该院第一位担任首席内科医师的女性。

text: She had been doing research at the Harvard School of Public Health.
zh: [10] 她当时正在哈佛公共卫生学院做研究。

text: She was asked to find out more about
zh: [11] 她被要求进一步调查

text: the foam that forms in the lungs of people with a condition called pulmonary edema.
zh: [12] 一种叫做肺水肿的病症患者肺部形成的泡沫。

text: At night, she worked in a hospital delivery room.
zh: [13] 晚上,她在医院产房工作。

text: She saw many premature babies with a disease now called respiratory distress syndrome.
zh: [14] 她见到许多患有如今称为呼吸窘迫综合征的早产儿。

text: She examined the lungs of babies who had died.
zh: [15] 她检查了夭折婴儿的肺部。

text: She found there was no air in their lungs, and she discovered why.
zh: [16] 她发现他们的肺里没有空气,并且找到了原因。

text: In her words, the material that was important,
zh: [17] 用她的话说,那种重要的物质,

text: the foam, was missing, and they were struggling to re -inflate their lungs.
zh: [18] 也就是泡沫,不见了,他们正努力让肺重新充气。

text: Nature put this foam or surfactant in the lungs to lower surface tension.
zh: [19] 大自然把这种泡沫,也就是表面活性物质,放在肺里以降低表面张力。

text: You cannot keep air spaces inflated without it.
zh: [20] 没有它,气囊就无法保持充气状态。

text: Babies usually develop this coating while they are in the womb, but many premature babies do not.
zh: [21] 婴儿通常在子宫内就会形成这种物质,但许多早产儿却没有。

text: Finally, in 1980, a Japanese doctor, Tetsuro Fujiwara,
zh: [22] 最终,在1980年,一位日本医生藤原哲郎

text: published a study about an artificial surfactant.
zh: [23] 发表了一篇关于人工表面活性物质的研究。

text: It could be given to a baby and within minutes the baby could breathe.
zh: [24] 这种物质可以用于婴儿,几分钟内婴儿就能呼吸。

text: The medical community had taken years to accept Dr. Avery's discovery,
zh: [25] 医学界花了多年才接受艾弗里博士的发现,

text: but she told Harvard Medical School in 1982 that she never gave up.
zh: [26] 但她在1982年告诉哈佛医学院,她从未放弃。

text: In her words, knowing what you want to do is important, especially in research.
zh: [27] 用她的话说,知道自己想做什么很重要,尤其是在做研究时。

text: Dr. Ann Hansen at Children's Hospital Boston
zh: [28] 波士顿儿童医院的安·汉森医生

text: remembers the first time she heard about Dr. Avery.
zh: [29] 还记得她第一次听说艾弗里博士的情形。

text: It was in 1990, when the government was in the process
zh: [30] 那是1990年,当时政府正在

text: of approving a surfactant called ExoSurf.
zh: [31] 批准一种名为ExoSurf的表面活性物质。

text: The doctor she was working with had some exciting news for her. She said it was the last night
zh: [32] 与她共事的医生带来了一个令人兴奋的消息。她说这是最后一晚,

text: before the hospital would start giving ExoSurf to all its preterm babies.
zh: [33] 之后医院就将开始为所有早产儿使用ExoSurf。

text: And then he told her the story of Mary Ellen Avery.
zh: [34] 然后他向她讲述了玛丽·艾伦·艾弗里的故事。

text: Dr. Avery was 84 years old when she died late last year.
zh: [35] 艾弗里博士去年年底去世,享年84岁。

text: For VOA Special English, I'm Mario Ritter.
zh: [36] 这里是美国之音特别英语,我是马里奥·里特。
